mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending expectations, driving negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in real economic activity, which compresses corporate revenue growth expectations and depresses equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint decline signal fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the economy operates in a high-inflation regime, subsequent increases in the federal funds rate force a compression of equity risk premiums, generating negative forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-inflation periods do not exhibit a statistically significant negative correlation with rate hikes (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly scales corporate earnings expectations, producing a persistent positive relationship with equity returns over the subsequent trading month as analysts update consensus forecasts.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or forward returns show no significant correlation with GDP growth surprises.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite positive signal, or forward returns in triggered months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit outstanding expands rapidly alongside rising housing starts, it indicates accelerating household leverage and real activity, which historically precedes positive equity returns over a one-month horizon as earnings momentum builds.
failure_condition: The joint expansion signal fails to predict positive forward returns in more than 50% of observations over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where industrial production peaks and unemployment falls below 5.0 percent, supply constraints dominate demand growth, compressing profit margins and generating negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (UNRATE < 5.0% and INDPRO at 12-month high)
failure_condition: Forward returns in late-cycle periods do not exhibit a statistically significant negative correlation with peak industrial output (p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.
